name: Senda de la Ermita de Casuar — circular desde Valdevacas de Montejo: Parque natural Hoces del río Riaza
region_intro: Hiking around Valdevacas De Montejo offers access to the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park, an area defined by dramatic river-carved canyons and gorges. The landscape features impressive rock formations, the flowing Riaza River, and diverse ecosystems including juniper and holm oak forests alongside lush riparian vegetation. These natural features provide varied terrain for hiking, from valley floors to higher slopes. Best hiking trails around Valdevacas De Montejo The most popular hiking route is Natural Park of the Hoces de Rio Riaza — circular from Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela, a 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route explores the core of the natural park, offering views of the river gorges. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda de la Ermita de Casuar — circular desde Valdevacas de Montejo: Parque natural Hoces del río Riaza, a moderate 6.8 miles (10.9 km) path. This trail starts directly from Valdevacas de Montejo and leads to the ruins of the Romanesque hermitage of El Casuar. Local hikers also love the Pantano de Linares y Maderuelo — circular, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain around the Linares reservoir and the historic village of Maderuelo, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Hiking around Valdevacas De Montejo is defined by deep canyons, the Riaza River, and diverse forests of juniper and holm oak. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging trails through the gorges. The routes in Valdevacas De Montejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Valdevacas De Montejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Valdevacas De Montejo?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes around Valdevacas De Montejo, offering a variety of experiences within the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park. These trails cater to different preferences, from easy riverside paths to more challenging routes through the gorges.

Q: What makes hiking in Valdevacas De Montejo unique?
A: The area is defined by the dramatic gorges and canyons carved by the Riaza River within the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park. Hikers can experience diverse ecosystems, from juniper and holm oak forests on the plateaus to lush riparian vegetation along the river. It's also renowned for its rich birdlife, particularly its large colony of griffon vultures.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Valdevacas De Montejo offers several easy hiking options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155078201/pantano-de-linares-y-maderuelo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Linares y Maderuelo — circular&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.3-mile (7.0 km) trail that explores the Linares reservoir and the historic village of Maderuelo. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153664073/hoces-del-riaza-circular-desde-montejo-de-la-vega-de-la-serrezuela-natural-park-hoces-del-rio-riaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Riaza — circular desde Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.6 miles (7.4 km) long.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in the area?
A: While many routes are moderate or easy, the rugged terrain of the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park does offer more demanding sections. There are 2 difficult routes available, providing opportunities for experienced hikers to explore the deeper canyons and more varied elevations.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Valdevacas De Montejo are circular, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. Popular circular routes include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036582116/natural-park-of-the-hoces-de-rio-riaza-circular-from-montejo-de-la-vega-de-la-serrezuela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the Hoces de Rio Riaza — circular from Montejo de la Vega de la Serrezuela&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1153647135/senda-de-la-ermita-de-casuar-circular-desde-valdevacas-de-montejo-parque-natural-hoces-del-rio-riaza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de la Ermita de Casuar — circular desde Valdevacas de Montejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hikers will encounter impressive gorges and canyons, the flowing Riaza River, and diverse vegetation including juniper and holm oak forests, as well as lush riverside ecosystems. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5327014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cliffs of the Hoces del Río Riaza&lt;/a&gt; are a particularly striking natural monument.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can hike to the ruins of the Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5327016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Martín del Casuar&lt;/a&gt;. Nearby, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3599924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maderuelo Historic Village and Medieval Walls&lt;/a&gt; offer a glimpse into the past, and the village of Castillejo de Robledo features Templar castle remains. Valdevacas de Montejo itself showcases traditional serrano architecture.

Q: Is Valdevacas De Montejo a good place for birdwatching?
A: Absolutely. The Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park is a significant ornithological site, home to one of Europe's largest colonies of griffon vultures. Other birds of prey like peregrine falcons and golden eagles can also be spotted. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3384644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birds of Prey Refuge&lt;/a&gt; is a testament to the area's importance for raptor conservation.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Valdevacas De Montejo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic river-carved landscapes, the opportunity to observe diverse wildlife, and the variety of trails that cater to different skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Valdevacas De Montejo?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Spring brings blooming wildflowers, while autumn showcases beautiful fall foliage. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially when exploring the shaded gorges.

Q: Are the hiking trails dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in the Hoces del Río Riaza Natural Park are suitable for dogs, provided they are kept under control, especially due to the presence of wildlife and livestock. It's always recommended to check specific trail regulations and respect local guidelines regarding pets in natural park areas.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer views of the Linares Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1155078201/pantano-de-linares-y-maderuelo-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de Linares y Maderuelo — circular&lt;/a&gt; route specifically leads through varied terrain around the Linares reservoir, offering impressive views of the water and the surrounding landscape, including the historic village of Maderuelo.
tips: The church of San Martín del Casuar is a Romanesque church currently in ruins and included in the Red List of endangered heritage in Spain. Its name comes from the town of Covasuar, where it was originally located. The church was built in the 11th century
Beautiful path that winds along one of the slopes that reaches the Río Riaza valley and allows us to see how its meanders meander through these fluvial terraces made by the river itself.

103 km from Segovia, on the border with the provinces of Burgos and Soria, we find a typical mountain town. It has a privileged environment, created by the Riaza River, which was declared a Natural Park.

waypoints.name: Church of San Martín del Casuar
